title: galium mollugo var. erectum
intro: Galium mollugo var. erectum, also known as the prickly bedstraw, is a perennial plant species in the Rubiaceae family, characterized by its erect stems and small white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections. The health benefits of Galium mollugo var. erectum include its potential to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic, helping to remove excess fluids from the body. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
